Package org.solenopsis.soap.partner

Class MatchRecord

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.partner.MatchRecord
public class MatchRecord extends Object

Java class for MatchRecord complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="MatchRecord">
   <complexContent>
     <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
       <sequence>
         <element name="additionalInformation" type="{urn:partner.soap.sforce.com}AdditionalInformationMap" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="fieldDiffs" type="{urn:partner.soap.sforce.com}FieldDiff" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="matchConfidence" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}double"/>
         <element name="record" type="{urn:sobject.partner.soap.sforce.com}sObject"/>
       </sequence>
     </restriction>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • getAdditionalInformation

      public List<AdditionalInformationMap> getAdditionalInformation()
      Gets the value of the additionalInformation property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the additionalInformation property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getAdditionalInformation().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list AdditionalInformationMap

      Returns:
      The value of the additionalInformation property.

    • getFieldDiffs

      public List<FieldDiff> getFieldDiffs()
      Gets the value of the fieldDiffs property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the fieldDiffs property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getFieldDiffs().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FieldDiff

      Returns:
      The value of the fieldDiffs property.

    • getMatchConfidence

      public double getMatchConfidence()
      Gets the value of the matchConfidence property.

    • setMatchConfidence

      public void setMatchConfidence(double value)
      Sets the value of the matchConfidence property.

    • setRecord

      Sets the value of the record property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is SObject
